title: Interpreting a mix of `parameter`/`localparam`/omitted in a _parameter_port_list_
body:
This is not a Verilator issue, more a SV standard inconsistency, where Verilator could choose one of the two interpretations.

I noticed differing interpretations in two tools, and I am not sure the SystemVerilog 2023 standard is clear about the correct interpretation.

The Questa simulator and Vivado synthesis tool interpret this _parameter_port_list_ differently:
```
module test #(
    localparam int unsigned XLEN = 32,
    int unsigned DATA_WIDTH = XLEN,
    int unsigned ADDR_WIDTH = 24,
    ...
)(
    ...
);
```

The Questa simulator will see `DATA_WIDTH` as a `parameter` which can be overriden during instantiation. Basically  interpreting the keyword omission as a `parameter`.

The Vivado Synthesis tool will see `DATA_WIDTH` as a `localparam`, as if when the keyword is omitted, the last included keyword is used. I did a few experiments and this seems to be the behavior. This it similar to port definitions, where the type is defined once for many signals:
```
    input logic [32-1:0] data_a, data_b,
    ...
```

The SystemVerilog 2023 standard only mentions this in one sentence "The parameter keyword can be omitted in a parameter port list." in the section "6.20.1 Parameter declaration syntax". Maybe there is more elsewhere, but I did not really read everything.

The workaround is simple, it just adds a bit of verbosity. Never omit the `parameter`/`localparam` keywords.
